explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in S tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in S tier with an overall score of 67 from a base nutrition score of 63. Context lifted it by 4 points.

topPros.title: fermented-food angle adds extra value
topPros.explanation: That gives it a practical benefit beyond the visible protein and fat numbers alone.

topPros.title: quite filling for the calories
topPros.explanation: The protein density gives it strong practical satiety value.

topPros.title: easy high-protein staple
topPros.explanation: Very easy to build meals or snacks around.

topCons.title: saturated fat is not trivial
topCons.explanation: Still much cleaner than weak dairy foods, but not ultra-low-fat.

topCons.title: not suitable for everyone
topCons.explanation: Lactose tolerance and dairy tolerance vary.

topCons.title: healthiness can shift with toppings
topCons.explanation: Sugary add-ins can change the practical outcome fast.

narrationNotes: Context improved the final tier case by 4 points.

closing.summary: Greek Yogurt has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when the protein payoff or fermentation angle outweighs the fat and sodium tradeoffs.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when the protein payoff or fermentation angle outweighs the fat and sodium tradeoffs.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
